preamble: Monophthongization of 'au' to 'u' involves the simplification of the diphthong into a single high back vowel, often as the second element assimilates the first.

examples.note: Common historical monophthongization where diphthongs simplify to high vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
